title: SkyeRoutexFlow + Platform House Circle — Master Walkthrough
summary: This is the full end-to-end walkthrough for the current SkyeRoutexFlow codebase, covering shell navigation, Routex core operations, Platform House Circle hospitality, automation, execution mesh, live ops, cloud sync, security, valuation, walkthrough discoverability, docs, portability, and the remaining live-environment boundary. The current build also includes an additive Neon enterprise backup lane for SQL-grade snapshot durability and restore.

sections.title: Platform shell and navigation spine
sections.summary: One shell now exposes Routex, Platform House Circle, valuation, walkthrough, sync, and operator-control surfaces instead of hiding them as side files.

sections.title: Routex core route and field execution engine
sections.summary: The Routex core remains the operating spine for route creation, stop execution, proof motion, and task spillover from integrated domains.

sections.title: Proof vault, export, and handoff system
sections.summary: The stack turns activity into evidence with exports, handoff packets, receipts, proof lanes, and white-glove delivery artifacts.

sections.title: AE, CRM, and account intelligence lane
sections.summary: The platform also carries client/account motion and follow-up logic, so it is not only a route runner.

sections.title: Platform House Circle hospitality domain
sections.summary: House Circle now lives inside the same stack as a first-class hospitality/member/event/campaign/POS domain.

sections.title: Join packets, QR redemption, and check-in lane
sections.summary: The platform can issue join packets, redeem them through QR or manual flow, and write guest/member/timeline/audit updates.

sections.title: POS ingest and sales intelligence lane
sections.summary: Sales data can be logged or ingested, then used to update revenue, guest spend, service cases, and operational follow-through.

sections.title: Service cases, automation rules, and playbooks
sections.summary: Signals can create service cases and Routex tasks through automation rules and playbooks instead of sitting idle in records.

sections.title: Dispatch shifts, assignments, and readiness execution mesh
sections.summary: V62 added shifts, assignments, readiness templates/runs, and escalation behavior, plus replica export/import and merge preview.

sections.title: Scanner, adapters, webhook inbox, jobs, and replay mesh
sections.summary: V63 moved the product into live-ops territory with scanning, adapters, webhooks, queues, dead-letter replay, and local realtime sync behavior.

sections.title: Cloud sync mesh and server-side control plane
sections.summary: V64 introduced signed sessions, state push/pull, frame ingest, cloud sync, and outbox replay so the platform could coordinate beyond one device.

sections.title: MFA, recovery, trusted devices, locks, and event feed
sections.summary: V65 added operator MFA, recovery codes, trusted devices, resource locks, release flow, and event-feed coordination.

sections.title: Investor valuation center
sections.summary: V66 made the valuation a live product surface with HTML/MD/JSON exports, cloud sync, health reporting, and nav discoverability.

sections.title: Master walkthrough center
sections.summary: V67 closes the “pieces not one whole” gap with one deep master walkthrough that explains the entire codebase lane by lane.

sections.title: Docs, deploy guides, directives, and smoke receipts
sections.summary: The repo ships layered implementation directives, deploy guides, status files, and smoke outputs across the upgrade passes.

sections.title: Import, export, replica, merge, and portability lanes
sections.summary: The stack supports portable bundles, replica previews, merge logic, and cross-environment carry instead of only one locked local state.

sections.title: What is finished versus what still needs live environment work
sections.summary: The remaining gap is mostly live deployment, real credentials, and final production storage choice, not missing major codebase architecture.

sections.title: Neon enterprise backup lane
sections.summary: V69 adds an additional enterprise-grade SQL lane without replacing the shipped file-backed persistence: schema, snapshot backup push/pull handlers, health reporting, restore flow, and a discoverable UI control surface.
where: Neon lane toolbar/card in the live shell
neon/ schema and deploy guide artifacts
whatYouCanDo: Push the current server state into Neon as a durable snapshot
Pull the latest Neon snapshot back into the platform
Inspect Neon readiness, schema, and last snapshot metadata from the live product
outcomes: Enterprise-grade backup and restore exists as an additional lane
The platform keeps its shipped file-backed lane while adding SQL-grade resilience

notes: This master walkthrough exists to replace scattered explanation with one operator-readable record.
It is shipped in the repo, surfaced in the live shell, and syncable to the platform cloud store.
Live deployment, external credentials, and final permanent backend selection still sit outside the walkthrough itself.
